title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are frustrating, especially when permissions haven't changed.

To help us narrow this down: could you walk us through the exact steps you take when the 403 appears? For example:

- Are you logging in fresh, or are you already logged in when it happens?
- Is it a specific URL (e.g., `/dashboard`) or does it happen on other pages too?
- Have you noticed any pattern — like it happening after being idle for a while, or after performing a specific action?

Even if it feels random, the sequence of steps leading up to it can help us reproduce the issue on our end.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm usually already logged in when it happens. It's not just the dashboard — it happens on other pages too, like when I go to view reports or project settings. There's no specific action that triggers it, it just seems random. Sometimes I'll click on something and get a 403, then I refresh the page and it works fine. If I had to guess I'd say it works maybe 2 out of 3 times? I figured it was some kind of network issue on our end but my coworker who also just got set up with the new analyst role is seeing the same thing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for those details — the pattern of it happening across multiple pages and clearing on refresh is really helpful. The mention of the new analyst role is an interesting clue we'll want to dig into.

Could you share some environment details? Specifically:

- What **browser** (and version) are you using?
- What **OS** are you on?
- What **version of TaskFlow** are you running (you can usually find this in Settings → About)?
- Is this a cloud-hosted instance or self-hosted?

This will help us figure out whether it's tied to a specific environment or something server-side.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm using Chrome, not sure of the exact version but it's pretty recent — I have auto-updates on. I'm on Windows 10. I think TaskFlow is on version 2.3.1 but I'd have to double-check. It's self-hosted — our IT team runs it internally behind a load balancer.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users with analyst role behind load balancer","problem":"Users with the newly created analyst role are experiencing intermittent 403 Forbidden errors across all pages (dashboard, reports, project settings). The errors appear randomly on roughly 1 in 3 page loads and clear on refresh. Multiple users with the same role are affected. The issue began a couple of days ago, coinciding with the new analyst role being assigned.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The self-hosted instance runs behind a load balancer with multiple backend servers. The analyst role permissions were likely added or updated recently, but not all backend nodes have consistent permission/role configuration or caches. When a request routes to a node with stale or missing analyst role permissions, a 403 is returned; on refresh, the load balancer may route to a correctly configured node. Alternatively, a session-affinity or authorization-cache inconsistency across nodes is causing some requests to lose role context.","reproduction_steps":["Log in as a user with the analyst role on the self-hosted TaskFlow instance","Navigate to any page (dashboard, reports, project settings)","Observe that approximately 1 in 3 page loads returns a 403 Forbidden error","Refresh the page — it loads successfully","Repeat navigation to see the intermittent pattern"],"environment":"Windows 10, Chrome (latest, auto-updated), TaskFlow ~2.3.1, self-hosted behind a load balancer","severity":"high","impact":"All users with the analyst role are affected. Core functionality (dashboard, reports, project settings) is intermittently inaccessible, blocking normal workflow roughly one-third of the time.","recommended_fix":"1. Check whether all backend nodes behind the load balancer have the same analyst role/permission configuration — look for config drift or failed deployments across nodes. 2. Inspect the authorization/session caching layer for consistency issues (e.g., role lookups that are cached per-node). 3. Review server-side access logs correlated with 403 responses to identify which backend node(s) are rejecting requests and compare their role/permission state. 4. Verify that the analyst role was fully propagated to all nodes when it was created.","proposed_test_case":"Send repeated authenticated requests as an analyst-role user to protected endpoints across a configurable number of iterations, asserting that all return 200. Run this against each backend node individually (bypassing the load balancer) to identify which node(s) return 403s.","information_gaps":["Exact TaskFlow version (reporter said ~2.3.1 but was unsure)","Server-side error logs or access logs showing the 403 responses","Number of backend nodes behind the load balancer","Whether non-analyst-role users also experience the issue (would confirm or rule out role-specific cause)","Exact date the analyst role was created/assigned relative to when the issue started"]}
